channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Michael Dell courted Trump early. His company has reaped rewards
item.description: The dynamic is emblematic of how business is seeking to sow favor with the president in his second term, and has departed from the norms of big business philanthropy.

item.title: Nvidia is investing billions into this emerging technology that could change the AI industry
item.description: Photonics is considered to be a more efficient alternative to the current process of transferring data using electricity, which could be crucial to the AI boom.

item.title: Infighting, court battles could put long-hyped air taxi breakthrough in jeopardy
item.description: Trump's eVTOL pilot program is giving the air taxi industry its validation, but legal battles have put investor sentiment on hold.

item.title: CNBC's Official Global Soccer Team Valuations 2026: Here's how the top 30 clubs in the world stack up
item.description: The world's 30 most valuable soccer teams are worth an average of $2.66 billion, according to CNBC's official team valuations.

item.title: Trump's Oman outburst throws 'Switzerland of the Middle East' into the spotlight
item.description: The Trump administration has threatened sanctions and military action against Oman, a longtime ally and close security partner.

item.title: Snowflake just had its best day ever. HSBC thinks there’s more to go
item.description: The bank upgraded the cloud computing name to buy from hold.

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on launchpad during ground test
item.description: Jeff Bezos' rocket maker suffered a setback on Thursday as its New Glenn rocket went up in flames.

item.title: Stock futures are little changed as traders eye developments in Middle East: Live updates
item.description: Boosted by a rally in the tech sector, all three major averages rose to fresh closing highs on Thursday.

item.title: NATO chief says alliance ‘ready to defend every inch of Allied territory’ after Russian drone hits member state Romania
item.description: Romania’s president said the collision demands a “firm, coordinated, and appropriate response."

item.title: Iran reportedly launches missiles as Trump mulls deal to pause war for two months
item.description: The latest military and economic actions come as President Trump insists he feels no pressure to make a deal with Iran before November's midterm elections.

item.title: Exxon warns oil inventories will hit dangerously low levels in weeks, forcing prices to shoot higher
item.description: The price of physical Brent oil cargoes will spike to $150 to $160 per barrel once inventories hit all-time lows, Exxon executive Neil Chapman said.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz shutdown: A visual guide to world's most critical oil chokepoint
item.description: Data shows how the outbreak of the Iran war in February and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz saw trade grind to a halt, triggering a severe global energy shock.

item.title: Costco says it saw 'record-breaking' gas volumes amid rising prices
item.description: Costco saw "recording-breaking volumes" of gas sales and said rising fuel prices drove first-time members to its stations.

item.title: Gap shares tumble 14% as retailer cuts sales guidance after disappointing Old Navy performance
item.description: Gap issued mixed results as sales at its largest brand, Old Navy, fell short of estimates.

item.title: Tesla Robotaxi fleet in Texas less than one-tenth size of Waymo's, filings reveal
item.description: Tesla has registered 42 automated vehicles in its driverless Robotaxi service in Texas, putting it far behind Waymo in the state.

item.title: Okta jumps 8%, tops first-quarter results on agentic AI demand
item.description: CEO Todd McKinnon said Okta is "playing a long game" on AI and is allocating more resources to agentic tools.

item.title: Disney's ABC files early broadcast licenses renewal 'under protest' against the FCC
item.description: Disney said it filed renewals for eight broadcast station licenses "under protest," after the FCC launched an early review years ahead of schedule.

item.title: AI is boosting the stock market. But it's a threat to private credit
item.description: Software investments have been a cash cow for private credit, but AI is changing that.

item.title: American Eagle's Aerie is booming, but its namesake brand is lagging despite Sydney Sweeney ads
item.description: American Eagle beat Wall Street's expectations but sales at its namesake banner fell short of estimates after its latest Sydney Sweeney campaign.

item.title: Samsung's shares surge as much as 6% after company ships next-generation AI memory chip samples
item.description: Shares of Samsung Electronics surged after the company began shipping HBM4E chip samples to its customers globally.

item.title: Costco issues a lukewarm quarter, but delivers on the metric that matters most
item.description: It was not the best quarter, but far from bad.

item.title: New: Earn up to 90,000 bonus points with these limited-time Southwest credit card offers
item.description: These Southwest cards also offer free checked bags, annual bonuses and other perks that Southwest frequent flyers will love.

item.title: Jim Cramer says these 3 mistakes are keeping investors out of AI winners
item.description: CNBC’s Jim Cramer pointed to three reasons investors may be missing some of the market’s biggest AI winners.

item.title: Innovent Biologics shares rise 10% after pact with Pfizer of up to $10.5 billion
item.description: Innovent Biologics rose as much as 10%, after entering a strategic global licensing and collaboration agreement with Pfizer to develop oncology medicines.

item.title: Anthropic tops OpenAI as most valuable AI startup, nears $1 trillion valuation in latest round
item.description: Anthropic is now the most valuable AI company in Silicon Valley after a new $65 billion funding round.

item.title: LG Electronics' shares surge 24% after showing automotive innovations using Google tech
item.description: Shares of LG Electronics surged after it unveiled automotive innovations using Google technology.

item.title: South Korea's Kospi, Japan's Topix hit record highs as investors shrug off Iran tensions
item.description: Asia-Pacific markets rose as investors shrugged off renewed Iran tensions against ceasefire optimism.

item.title: Asia’s rich fear losing the family fortune — but many still have no succession plans, survey shows
item.description: Asia's wealthy families want to preserve their fortunes across generations, but many still lack basic succession plans, according to a new Lombard Odier survey.

item.title: Snowflake surges 36% for best day ever on AI frenzy, fueling software rally
item.description: The Snowflake rally also lifted shares of ServiceNow, Oracle and Palantir, while Salesforce bucked the trend.

item.title: Nokia doesn’t make phones anymore. So how does it make billions?
item.description: Once the world's dominant mobile phone maker, Nokia has rebuilt itself as a behind-the-scenes provider of the infrastructure that powers global connectivity. Today, the Finnish company generates billions in revenue not just from the equipment it sells to telecom operators, but also from a vast portfolio of patents used across the communications industry.
